Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Qiu, Maik Punke², Yuan Tian³, Ying Han³, Siqi Wang1, Yishi Su4, Marco Salvalaglio2,5,

Composition_Info.Note: Pure Al; FCC crystal structure. Grain boundary systems studied: Σ27 [110] ATGB, Σ11 [110] ATGB, Σ19 [110] ATGB, Σ25 [100] ATGB, Σ39 [111] STGB, ATGB (φθ = 9.8°), SMGB (φρ = 36.3°), AMGB, and low-angle Σ25 ATGB.

Process_Text.original: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations at 0.5 Tm (Al melting point); MD bicrystal configurations include Σ27 [110] ATGB (driven by γ), Σ11 [110] ATGB (φθ = 46.7°), Σ19 [110] ATGB, Σ25 [100] ATGB (driven by τ), Σ39 [111] STGB, ATGB (φθ = 9.8°), SMGB (φρ = 36.3°), and AMGB (equivalent to combining φθ = 9.8° and φρ = 36.3°); driving forces of at least three different values, each simulation repeated at least three times with different initial conditions; oscillatory driving force ψ = 15 × (−1)^{|t|} m eV/atom at frequency f = 1 ns⁻¹; oscillatory shear stress τ = 150 × (−1)^{|t|} MPa at f = 1 ns⁻¹; cyclic annealing simulations.
Process_Text.simplified: MD simulations of Al bicrystals at 0.5 Tm; multiple GB types (ATGB, STGB, SMGB, AMGB) driven by synthetic driving forces; oscillatory forcing experiments.

Microstructure_Text.original: ATGB structures dissociate into different constituent defects (e.g., twin boundary + another GB for Σ27; two sets of facets for Σ11; different facet sets for Σ19) when migrating in opposite directions; Σ25 low-angle ATGB migrates through glide of different sets of GB dislocations in opposite directions; directional migration involves dissociation of GB into two types of constituent defects with distinct energy barriers; disconnection nucleation, propagation, and annihilation analyzed via minimum energy paths (MEPs); ATGB MEPs are nonsymmetric.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: ATGBs dissociate into distinct constituent defects during migration; migration mechanism depends on driving force direction; disconnection glide controls migration; ATGB MEPs are nonsymmetric, giving direction-dependent mobility.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: MD at 0.5 Tm; Σ39 ATGB; oscillatory ψ
Properties_Info.Note: Reconstructed from |v+| − |v−| × 10 = 0.523 from MD data in Fig. 2G

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: PFC simulation; Σ39 [111] ATGB; cyclic annealing f = 3.7 × 10⁻³ s⁻¹
Properties_Info.Note: Unidirectional GB migration under cyclic annealing; consistent with oscillatory ψ MD result of 0.523 ± 0.204 m/s

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: MD at 0.6 Tm; oscillatory shear stress τ with zero mean and amplitude below yield strength; f = 1.0 ns⁻¹; 3D polycrystalline Al
Properties_Info.Note: Grain growth rate with oscillatory shear is ~2× that without oscillatory shear; no substantial dislocation plasticity or twinning observed

Composition_Info.Note: Pure Al; FCC crystal structure. Σ39 [111] STGB studied under PFC cyclic annealing and oscillatory forcing.

Process_Text.original: PFC simulations for Σ39 [111] STGB under cyclic annealing at frequency 3.7 × 10⁻³ s⁻¹; temperature cycling between T_high and T_low; oscillatory driving force ψ = 15 × (−1)^{|t|} m eV/atom at f = 1 ns⁻¹; oscillatory shear stress τ = 150 × (−1)^{|t|} MPa at f = 1 ns⁻¹.
Process_Text.simplified: PFC and MD simulations of Al Σ39 [111] STGB under oscillatory forcing and cyclic annealing; zero net drift expected.

Microstructure_Text.original: STGB is symmetric by both mirror (m') and 2'-fold rotation symmetries; disconnection structures and glide events for upward and downward terrace migration are related by mirror and 2'-fold symmetries; STGB MEP is symmetric about the midpoint of the path.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: STGB has symmetric disconnection structure; symmetric MEP leads to direction-independent GB mobility.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: MD for STGB and SMGB at 0.5–0.6 Tm; |v+/v−| ≈ 1 within standard deviation
Properties_Info.Note: STGB and SMGB show direction-independent mobility within statistical uncertainty

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: MD at 0.6 Tm; oscillatory ψ or τ with zero mean; Σ39 [111] STGB
Properties_Info.Note: STGB position fluctuates on oscillatory loading but mean velocity is zero (symmetric MEP)

Composition_Info.Note: Pure Cu; FCC crystal structure. Grain boundary systems: Σ27 [110] ATGB, Σ99 [110] STGB.

Process_Text.original: MD simulations of Cu bicrystals; Σ27 [110] ATGB driven by γ (free energy density jump); Σ99 [110] STGB driven by τ (shear stress); driving forces of at least three different values, each simulation repeated at least three times with different initial conditions.
Process_Text.simplified: MD simulations of Cu bicrystals at various driving forces; Σ27 ATGB driven by γ; Σ99 STGB driven by τ.

Microstructure_Text.original: Σ27 ATGB dissociates into a twin boundary and another GB when migrating in one direction while remaining undissociated when migration is in the opposite direction; Σ99 STGB is macroscopically symmetric but exhibits a 9R phase, microscopically nonsymmetric—the width of the 9R phase differs when migrating in different directions.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Σ27 ATGB shows structural dissociation during migration in one direction; Σ99 STGB has asymmetric 9R phase structure affecting migration.

Composition_Info.Note: Ni bicrystal; upper grain zone axis close to [112], lower grain zone axis close to [110]; grain boundary is nonsymmetric asymmetric tilt grain boundary (ATGB). FCC crystal structure.

Process_Text.original: In situ STEM observation of Ni bicrystal ATGB migration under constant and oscillatory annealing temperatures; sample initially annealed at constant 450°C (high temperature); then at constant 400±50°C; then cyclic annealing between 350°C and 450°C with frequency 0.33 Hz (from 20 to 129 min); GB position tracked over time.
Process_Text.simplified: In situ STEM of Ni bicrystal ATGB under constant and cyclic annealing (350–450°C, 0.33 Hz).

Microstructure_Text.original: Upper grain zone axis close to [112], lower grain zone axis close to [110]; grain boundary is nonsymmetric; migration observed in real time under in situ annealing conditions.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Nonsymmetric ATGB between [112] and [110] oriented grains; unidirectional migration observed under cyclic annealing.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 400±50°C; constant temperature annealing; Ni bicrystal ATGB
Properties_Info.Note: Small migration likely driven by surface energy difference of two grains

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Cyclic annealing 350–450°C, f = 0.33 Hz; Ni bicrystal ATGB
Properties_Info.Note: Unidirectional migration under cyclic annealing; ~5.61 ± 0.4 × 10⁻⁹ m/s; 200× faster than constant T migration; confirms GB Brownian ratchet behavior
